## OQS Technical Steering Committee

Rodrigo encouraged team members to engage with two open TSC issues before the next TSC meeting: the TSC vice-chair selection ([issue #295](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/295)) — on which Douglas has put himself forward as a candidate — and the removal of the security email list ([issue #293](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/293)). Rodrigo noted that the broader question of how to handle security reports going forward, beyond the email list itself, also needs discussion; input from the ongoing Trail of Bits collaboration (see below) is expected to inform that process.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (all):** Comment on the TSC vice-chair selection ([issue #295](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/295)) and the security email list removal ([issue #293](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/tsc/issues/293)).

## liboqs

### liboqs 0.16.0 release candidate 1 ([PR #2451](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2451))

Bruce reported that the RC1 pull request was marked ready for review last Friday. The only remaining unchecked item is the Markdown linter PR ([#2446](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2446)), which Bruce considers non-blocking for RC1. There are no hard blockers; the main remaining task is review, and the primary focus of that review should be proofreading the release notes. Some CI pipeline failures are present but are unrelated to any code change in the RC PR (the PR itself only changes version numbers and release notes) and are attributed to intermittent GitHub Actions issues.

Bruce confirmed he will include the mlkem-native v1.2.0 update ([PR #2470](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2470)) before tagging RC1, as this is a self-contained upstream update that amounts to rerunning `copy_from_upstream` and adding a release note entry — estimated at under an hour's work to be done immediately after the status meeting. The ppc64le backend work ([PR #2464](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2464)) associated with the mlkem-native update will not be included in 0.16, as it represents new platform support rather than a fix.

Regarding timing: Bruce is also managing the Trail of Bits collaboration this week. RC1 will be treated as a pre-release, giving the team a low-stakes checkpoint that can be rolled back if needed. Trail of Bits findings will target post-0.16 work and will not delay the RC.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (all):** Review [PR #2451](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2451) (liboqs 0.16.0 RC1), focusing on proofreading the release notes, as soon as possible.

### PR [#2407](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2407) — HQC update (merged)

The HQC PR has been merged. Rodrigo thanked Bruce for driving this to completion, as it was the main blocker for the 0.16 release.

### PR [#2431](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2431) — Value barrier for FrodoKEM (merged)

Merged. The release notes in RC1 include an elaborated section on the FrodoKEM naming change: the existing variant has been renamed to FrodoKEM-Ephemeral, and a new FrodoKEM-Standard variant is introduced. This addresses [issue #2405](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/issues/2405).

### PR [#2366](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2366) — External-µ signature variant for ML-DSA

This PR has been marked ready for review and represents a significant post-0.16 development. Norman raised the question of how to expose the External-µ API in oqs-provider (see provider section below). No one commented further at this stage beyond noting it as a priority item after the 0.16 release.

### Formal verification of ML-DSA (mldsa-native)

Rafael Misoczki (Meta) asked about plans for formally verified ML-DSA support, noting that Meta is tracking formally verified PQC implementations closely. Basil explained that mldsa-native currently includes CBMC-based formal verification of the C code, but formal verification of the assembly code is not yet complete. The goal of the final mldsa-native release (currently in beta) is full formal verification. Hart added that the mldsa-native team (Hanno and Matthias) expected to complete this within the next couple of weeks.

### PR [#2449](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2449) — Constant-time analysis framework

Pablo reported that he has opened a companion PR in the `ci-containers` repository to add the patched Valgrind (with the KyberSlash patch applied) as a container image, avoiding the need to rebuild Valgrind from source in CI on every run. That PR has already been approved. Once it merges, Pablo will clean up the documentation in PR #2449 (consistent with the agreement from the previous meeting) and move it to ready for review. Rodrigo asked whether Pablo had checked if Valgrind upstream intends to incorporate the KyberSlash patch natively; Pablo confirmed he had not yet done so and will follow up.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Pablo):** Check whether Valgrind upstream has plans to incorporate the KyberSlash patch natively, and report back.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Pablo):** Once the ci-containers PR merges, finalise documentation in [PR #2449](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2449) and move to ready for review.

## OQS-OpenSSL 3 provider

### PR [#778](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/pull/778) — Avoid encoder hotpath in P-curve hybrid KEM keygen

Rodrigo reiterated his concerns from last week: his own testing on an Intel machine was unable to reproduce the performance regression that the PR claims to address. Norman is in the process of attempting to reproduce the issue on a Mac (the platform originally reported by the PR author) and will report back. Rodrigo asked Michael and Norman to comment on the PR with their findings.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Norman):** Attempt to reproduce the performance issue on Mac and report findings on [PR #778](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/oqs-provider/pull/778).

### External-µ ML-DSA in the provider

Norman raised the question of how External-µ ML-DSA support (once [PR #2366](https://github.com/open-quantum-safe/liboqs/pull/2366) lands in liboqs) would be surfaced in oqs-provider. Rodrigo noted that OpenSSL 3.5 includes native ML-DSA support, and the provider currently defers to OpenSSL for standardised algorithms on 3.5+. Exposing External-µ via a provider parameter for pre-3.5 OpenSSL versions (analogous to how the provider exposes the quantum and post-quantum key parts separately today) seems a plausible approach, but the interaction with 3.5 behaviour — where the provider's ML-DSA implementation is not active — needs careful consideration, particularly for hybrid schemes. Rodrigo committed to reviewing how OpenSSL 3.5 handles the External-µ API and will bring a proposal to the next status meeting.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Rodrigo):** Review how OpenSSL 3.5 handles the External-µ ML-DSA API and bring a proposal for provider integration to the next status meeting.

### Post-0.16 provider release

Rodrigo noted that once liboqs 0.16 is released, a corresponding oqs-provider release will need to follow. This should be kept in mind in the coming weeks.

## Trail of Bits collaboration

Bruce began working with the Trail of Bits engineer this week. A kickoff meeting was held the previous day (Monday, June 22), in which the team was asked to identify areas of highest interest for security analysis. The engineer will communicate primarily via a dedicated Slack channel and will open GitHub issues or PRs for any findings; security-sensitive findings may be handled via security advisories rather than public PRs, to be decided case by case.

Hart confirmed that Trail of Bits is preparing a brief publication summarising their work with each open source project they engage with during this collaboration, and encouraged the team to welcome this as positive visibility. Rodrigo agreed and noted that he had asked Trail of Bits to share the text before publication; Hart offered to help amplify any OQS-authored blog post as well.

Norman asked to be added to the Slack channel. Rodrigo will contact the Trail of Bits project coordinator (Emily) to arrange this.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Action (Rodrigo):** Contact the Trail of Bits coordinator to add Norman to the collaboration Slack channel.
